signals.title: USD — Geopolitical Shock + Yield Curve
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.45 — DXY tumbled to a 2.5-month low following reports of a potential US-Iran memorandum. WTI Oil down -6.59% and US 10Y yields falling -1.40% to 4.354% remove the primary drivers of recent USD strength. Bull Flattener regime (+40.4bp 2s10s) persists but softening yields suggest a shift in the tightening narrative. USD/JPY 300bp carry advantage remains, but geopolitical de-escalation triggers rapid long-liquidation. Watch for a sustained break below 155.00 to confirm a trend reversal.

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hike Bias
signals.text: NZD/CAD at 0.4370 — NZD is the G8 leader (+0.592%) while CAD is the laggard (-0.530%), creating a 1.122% spread. NZD strength is supported by a hawkish RBNZ bias (Market bias: Hike) vs BoC on hold at 2.25%. NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (23% long) contradicts the hike bias, creating significant short-squeeze risk if the May 27 meeting confirms hawkishness. CAD remains under pressure from the -6.59% collapse in WTI oil prices.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7234 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) shows institutional conviction while retail sentiment is 90% short. This extreme divergence suggests retail is fading a strong institutional trend. RBA at 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% provides a +60bp carry advantage for AUD. Retail capitulation risk is high if price extends above 0.7300, potentially accelerating the move as shorts are squeezed.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1755 — Correlation with EuroStoxx at +0.734 vs norm of -0.135 (z=+1.88σ) indicates a significant regime shift where EUR is trading as a pro-cyclical risk asset rather than a funding currency. Despite the +175bp Fed-ECB carry disadvantage, EUR/USD is bid as US yields soften. Watch for the ECB meeting on 11 Jun to determine if the carry gap narrows.
